title: Finalize workspace_initialize(cwd) startup contract and repo-root invariants
summary: Define the one-time workspace initialization contract for v0.4.6 so the MCP server can start when process CWD is '/' under Zed multi-project launches, while preserving strict safety around project-root binding and artifact path resolution.
acceptance_criteria: Defines the startup contract for workspace_initialize(cwd) as the required first binding step when startup CWD is '/'.
Specifies repo-root lifecycle rules for unresolved startup, first successful bind, same-root reinitialization, and conflicting rebind rejection.
Clarifies backward-compatible behavior for normal non-root startup where the server can bind immediately from process CWD.
Preserves the safety invariant that '/' must never become the bound project root and that artifact paths must never resolve under '/.agent/'.
Identifies the runtime boundary where root-dependent tools must fail clearly until workspace initialization is complete.

steps.description: Review current startup assumptions in main.py, RepoContext validation, and JSON-RPC tool execution flow.
Define the workspace_initialize(cwd) contract, including input validation, return shape, and no-op behavior for same-root reinitialization.
Specify repo-root state transitions and invariants for unresolved startup, successful bind, and conflicting rebind attempts.
Document how root-dependent tools behave before initialization and how normal single-project startup remains backward compatible.

notes: This ticket is contract and design focused; it should not implement runtime changes directly.
The selected design assumes one MCP server instance corresponds to one stable Zed project directory for the duration of the session.
The contract should remain explicit enough that .rules can require workspace_initialize(cwd) before canonical state restore.
